reasoning.issue: The question asks what test underlies the build-up (Cumulative Amount) basket and when the basket begins accruing capacity.
reasoning.analysis:
The 'Cumulative Amount' (build-up basket) is defined as the sum of (without duplication) multiple additive sources, plus a 'greatest of' sub-aggregate among three income-based tests. The basket starts growing from the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs (growth_start_anchor: closing_date_fiscal_quarter_start; start_date_language from v3 entity: 'the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs').

The basket has the following structure:

1. STARTER AMOUNT (norm: 6e76ed06_builder_source_starter): The greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA for the most recently ended Test Period (cap_usd: $130M, cap_grower_pct: 100%, cap_grower_reference: consolidated_ebitda_ltm). This is a fixed floor that contributes via sum.

2. THREE-TEST GREATEST-OF SUB-AGGREGATE (norm: 6e76ed06_builder_source_three_test_aggregate): The basket uses a 'greatest_of' aggregation among three alternative income-based measures:
   a. CNI Source (6e76ed06_builder_source_cni): 50% of cumulative Consolidated Net Income since the anchor date.
   b. ECF Source (6e76ed06_builder_source_ecf): The 'Available Retained ECF Amount' — cumulative Excess Cash Flow not required to prepay Term Loans under Section 2.10(f) or other indebtedness under Section 2.10(h), floored at $0 per fiscal year.
   c. EBITDA FC Source (6e76ed06_builder_source_ebitda_fc): 140% of Consolidated EBITDA for the most recently ended Test Period (ebitda_fc_multiplier: 140%).

3. EQUITY PROCEEDS (6e76ed06_builder_source_other_equity_proceeds): 100% of equity proceeds (Not Otherwise Applied), added via sum.

4. RETAINED ASSET SALE PROCEEDS (6e76ed06_builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds): Net Cash Proceeds from asset sales retained after mandatory sweep obligations, added via sum.

5. INVESTMENT RETURNS (6e76ed06_builder_source_investment_returns): Returns on investments (dividends, principal repayments, etc.), added via sum.

The Cumulative Amount is reduced by prior usage for RP payments, Restricted Debt Payments, and Investments (reduced_by_rp_usage, reduced_by_rdp_usage, reduced_by_investment_usage = true), but is NOT reduced by unrestricted subsidiary designations.
reasoning.interactions: The three-test sub-aggregate (6e76ed06_builder_source_three_test_aggregate) contributes to the parent builder_usage_permission (6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission) via greatest_of aggregation at child_index 0. The CNI (child_index 2), ECF (child_index 3), and EBITDA FC (child_index 4) sources each contribute to the three-test aggregate via greatest_of. The starter, equity proceeds, retained asset sale proceeds, and investment returns sources contribute directly to the parent usage permission via sum. Asset sale proceeds flow into the retained asset sale proceeds source when retained per leverage-based sweep tier exemptions or below de minimis thresholds. The Cumulative Amount is shared with the RDP builder basket (6e76ed06_builder_rdp_basket_permission), subject to intercreditor restrictions.
reasoning.conclusion: The Cumulative Amount basket is based on a multi-source build-up structure anchored to the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs, with the income-based core determined by the greatest of 50% of cumulative CNI, the Available Retained ECF Amount, or 140% of Consolidated EBITDA (LTM), plus additive sources for the starter amount, equity proceeds, retained asset sale proceeds, and investment returns.

stage2.answer: The build-up basket is defined as the 'Cumulative Amount' [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p. 32] and begins accruing on the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs. The basket is built from: (i) a starter floor of the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA (LTM) [norm: 6e76ed06_builder_source_starter]; (ii) the greatest of (a) 50% of cumulative Consolidated Net Income [6e76ed06_builder_source_cni], (b) the Available Retained ECF Amount (cumulative Excess Cash Flow not swept to prepay debt under Section 2.10(f)/(h), floored at $0 per year) [6e76ed06_builder_source_ecf], and (c) 140% of Consolidated EBITDA for the most recently ended Test Period [6e76ed06_builder_source_ebitda_fc]; plus additive sources for (iii) 100% of equity proceeds Not Otherwise Applied [6e76ed06_builder_source_other_equity_proceeds], (iv) retained asset sale proceeds [6e76ed06_builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds], and (v) returns on investments [6e76ed06_builder_source_investment_returns]. The Cumulative Amount is reduced dollar-for-dollar by prior usage for Restricted Payments, Restricted Debt Payments, and Investments, but is not reduced by unrestricted subsidiary designations, and is shared with the RDP builder basket under Section 6.09(a)(A) subject to intercreditor restrictions.

questions.gold_answer: The Cumulative Amount is based on the greatest of three tests: (1) 50% of cumulative Consolidated Net Income (which amount shall not be less than zero for any fiscal quarter), (2) Excess Cash Flow not required to be applied to prepay Term Loans or any other debt (such amount cannot be less than $0 for any fiscal year), (3) cumulative Consolidated EBITDA minus 140% of cumulative Consolidated Fixed Charges. All tests start growing from the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs.

reasoning.issue: Whether the Borrower is permitted under the restricted payments covenant to distribute (by dividend or otherwise) the equity interests it holds in Unrestricted Subsidiaries to its equity holders.
reasoning.analysis: The primary norm is norm_id 6e76ed06_unrestricted_sub_equity_distribution_permission, extracted from the unsub_distribution_basket at Section 6.06(p) (p. 198). That basket expressly permits 'the distribution, by dividend or otherwise, of shares of Equity Interests of… an Unrestricted Subsidiary or the proceeds thereof.' The v3 attributes confirm: covers_equity_interests = true, is_categorical = true, capacity_category = 'categorical', partial_applicability = false. Because it is categorical, no dollar cap, ratio test, or builder-basket availability is required — the permission is unconditional on its face. The basket also covers indebtedness owed by the Unrestricted Subsidiary to Holdings or a Restricted Subsidiary, assets of the Unrestricted Subsidiary, and proceeds of any of the foregoing (covers_proceeds = true). Notably, reduces_investment_basket = false, so using this basket does not consume investment capacity. The rebuilds_builder_basket = true flag indicates that distributions made under this basket can replenish the Cumulative Amount (builder basket). The J.Crew blocker (norm 6e76ed06_jcrew_blocker_prohibition, Section 'Definition of Unrestricted Subsidiary,' p. 89) is relevant only at the designation stage — it prohibits designating a Restricted Subsidiary as Unrestricted if it would own Material IP — but does not restrict distributions of equity in an already-designated Unrestricted Subsidiary. The general RP basket (6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission, §6.06(j)) and ratio basket (6e76ed06_ratio_rp_basket_permission, §6.06(o)) are supplementary and not needed here because §6.06(p) provides a standalone categorical permission.
reasoning.interactions: The unsub distribution basket (§6.06(p)) stands alone as a categorical permission with no cap or condition, so the general RP basket (§6.06(j), capped at $130M / 100% EBITDA) and the ratio RP basket (§6.06(o), subject to First Lien Leverage Ratio ≤ 5.75x pro forma) are not necessary to support the distribution. The J.Crew blocker (§'Definition of Unrestricted Subsidiary') constrains designation of subsidiaries as Unrestricted but does not interact with distributions of equity in already-designated Unrestricted Subsidiaries. No defeaters are present for norm 6e76ed06_unrestricted_sub_equity_distribution_permission.
reasoning.conclusion: Yes — Section 6.06(p) provides a categorical, uncapped permission for the Borrower to distribute (by dividend or otherwise) equity interests in Unrestricted Subsidiaries, with no ratio test, dollar cap, or builder-basket condition required.

stage2.answer: Yes. Section 6.06(p) (p. 198) expressly and categorically permits the distribution, by dividend or otherwise, of shares of Equity Interests of an Unrestricted Subsidiary (or the proceeds thereof) without any dollar cap, ratio condition, or builder-basket availability requirement. The basket also covers indebtedness owed by the Unrestricted Subsidiary and its assets. This permission does not reduce investment basket capacity and, notably, any such distribution rebuilds the Cumulative Amount (builder basket). The J.Crew blocker [Section 'Definition of Unrestricted Subsidiary,' p. 89] applies only at the designation stage and does not limit distributions of equity in already-designated Unrestricted Subsidiaries.

reasoning.issue: The question asks whether any investment, restricted debt payment (RDP), or other baskets can be reallocated and used to make restricted payments or dividends.
reasoning.analysis:
There are three key reallocation pathways identified in the data:

1. **Builder Basket (Cumulative Amount) — shared across RP, RDP, and Investments**: The Cumulative Amount (builder basket) under the Definition of Cumulative Amount [p.32] is a single shared pool used for dividends/RPs (Section 6.06), RDPs (Section 6.09(a)(A)), and investments (Section 6.03(x)). The builder_rdp_basket (`6e76ed06_builder_rdp_basket_permission`) expressly shares capacity with the RP builder basket (`shares_with_rp_builder: true`). The builder basket is reduced by RP usage, RDP usage, and investment usage (`reduced_by_rp_usage: true`, `reduced_by_rdp_usage: true`, `reduced_by_investment_usage: true`). This means the Cumulative Amount is a fungible pool across all three covenant categories — capacity used for one reduces availability for the others, and vice versa.

2. **General RDP Basket → General RP Basket (fungible pool)**: The general RDP basket under Section 6.09(a)(I) (`6e76ed06_general_rdp_basket_permission`) has `action_scope: reallocable` and capacity of the greater of $130M / 100% Consolidated EBITDA. The general RP basket under Section 6.06(j) (`6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission`) also has `action_scope: reallocable` and the same cap, with `capacity_composition: fungible`. These two baskets share the same dollar cap (greater of $130M / 100% EBITDA) and are reallocable to each other — capacity available under the RDP general basket can be applied to restricted payments and vice versa.

3. **Investment Basket 6.03(y) references RDP capacity**: The investment pathway under Section 6.03(y) (`investment_pathway_3`) explicitly references RDP capacity — its cap equals the greater of $130M/100% EBITDA **plus** all amounts available under Section 6.09(a) (the RDP baskets). This cross-references RDP basket capacity into the investment basket, though the directionality here flows RDP capacity into investments, not directly back to RP.

4. **Investment Basket 6.03(x) — Cumulative Amount**: Investments may be made up to the Cumulative Amount under Section 6.03(x), which is the same builder pool used for RP/RDP — fully fungible/shared.
reasoning.interactions: The builder basket (`6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission`) is the central node: it contributes to RP (6.06), RDP (6.09(a)(A)), and investments (6.03(x)) simultaneously, with usage under any one covenant reducing capacity for the others. The general RP basket (6.06(j)) and general RDP basket (6.09(a)(I)) are tagged `reallocable` with fungible composition and the same dollar cap, forming a shared pool. No defeaters are present in the data.
reasoning.conclusion: Yes — the Cumulative Amount (builder basket) is explicitly shared across RP, RDP, and investment baskets, and the general RP and general RDP baskets share a fungible capacity pool (each capped at the greater of $130M / 100% Consolidated EBITDA), making them mutually reallocable.

stage2.answer: Yes, there are multiple reallocation pathways. First, the Cumulative Amount (builder basket, Definition of Cumulative Amount, p. 32) is a single shared pool usable for restricted payments [Section 6.06], restricted debt payments [Section 6.09(a)(A)], and investments [Section 6.03(x)]; usage in any category reduces availability in all others. Second, the general RP basket [Section 6.06(j), p. 196] and the general RDP basket [Section 6.09(a)(I), p. 200] each carry a cap of the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA and are marked as reallocable with fungible composition — capacity under either basket may be applied to the other. Third, the investment basket under Section 6.03(y) expressly incorporates all amounts available under Section 6.09(a) (the full RDP basket stack) as incremental investment capacity, linking RDP availability directly to investment headroom. The builder RDP basket [Section 6.09(a)(A), p. 199] expressly shares its pool with the RP builder basket (subject to applicable intercreditor agreements and subordination terms).

reasoning.issue: Whether asset sale proceeds can be used to fund dividends, and if so, under what mechanism and conditions.
reasoning.analysis:
The agreement contains a defined 'Cumulative Amount' (builder basket) that aggregates multiple sources of capacity for restricted payments, including dividends. Norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds establishes that retained net cash proceeds from asset sales constitute one additive source (child_index 6) flowing into the Cumulative Amount. The proceeds_flow edge confirms this: net cash proceeds from asset sales subject to mandatory prepayment under Section 2.10(c) flow into clause (f) of the Cumulative Amount when retained — i.e., when not swept to prepay debt under the leverage-based sweep tiers or when below de minimis thresholds.

The sweep mechanics work as follows: if First Lien Leverage Ratio > 5.75x, 100% of net cash proceeds must be applied to prepay term loans (sweep_tier_100pct); if between 5.50x and 5.75x, 50% must be swept (sweep_tier_50pct); if ≤ 5.50x, 0% sweep is required (sweep_tier_0pct). The portion not required to be swept — the 'retained' portion — can enter the Cumulative Amount. Additionally, individual and annual de minimis thresholds (individual: greater of $[not specified in individual_de_minimis_usd — only individual_de_minimis_pct: 0.3%] and annual: greater of $40mm and 0.3% of Consolidated EBITDA) carve out certain proceeds from the sweep obligation entirely.

Once in the Cumulative Amount, norm 6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission confirms that the aggregate Cumulative Amount (a general_purpose basket) may be applied to make dividend payments (norm_scopes_action: make_dividend_payment), among other restricted payments. The builder basket is reduced by RP usage, RDP usage, and investment usage (reduced_by_rp_usage: true), so drawdowns for dividends reduce remaining capacity for other uses.
reasoning.interactions: The asset_sale_proceeds source (6e76ed06_builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds) contributes additively (sum) to the parent norm 6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission via child_index 6. The sweep tiers (6e76ed06_sweep_tier_100pct, _50pct, _0pct) are threshold gates that determine what portion of proceeds is 'retained' and therefore eligible to enter the Cumulative Amount. The builder_usage_permission then permits dividend payments up to the aggregate Cumulative Amount. No defeaters are present in the data.
reasoning.conclusion: Yes — the portion of asset sale net cash proceeds not required to be swept to prepay term loans (determined by the leverage-based sweep tiers) enters the Cumulative Amount builder basket, which expressly permits dividend payments.

stage2.answer: Yes, asset sale proceeds can be used to make dividends, but only indirectly and subject to the leverage-based sweep mechanics. The net cash proceeds of asset sales that are not required to be applied to prepay term loans under Section 2.10(c) — i.e., proceeds retained after applying the applicable sweep percentage (100% if First Lien Leverage Ratio > 5.75x [Section 2(a), p.5]; 50% if between 5.50x–5.75x [Section 2(b), p.5]; 0% if ≤ 5.50x [Section 2(c), p.6]) — flow into the 'Cumulative Amount' builder basket as an additive source [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32]. The Cumulative Amount expressly permits dividend payments as a permitted use [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32], subject to the basket being reduced by prior RP, RDP, and investment usage. Additionally, asset sale proceeds below the de minimis thresholds (annual: greater of $40mm and 0.3% of Consolidated EBITDA) are exempt from the sweep and can similarly enter the basket [Definition of Prepayable Net Cash Proceeds, p.71].

Also includes asset sale proceeds not swept when First Lien Net Leverage Ratio is 5.75x or less (50% of proceeds) or 5.50x or less (100% of proceeds). Also includes Net Cash Proceeds from non-collateral assets, ordinary course asset sales, asset sales from non-ratio baskets, casualty events, and proceeds from collateral assets below de minimis thresholds of $20M/15% EBITDA individual and $40M/30% EBITDA annual.

reasoning.analysis:
Step 1 — Classify baskets by capacity_category:

(A) GENERAL_PURPOSE baskets (sum independently unless fungible/shared):
• General RP Basket [Section 6.06(j)]: greater of $130M and 100% Consolidated EBITDA. capacity_composition = 'fungible' — this basket shares a pool with the general RDP basket under Section 6.09(a)(I) (both are $130M / 100% EBITDA), but the norms do not show a shares_capacity_pool link between them. Absent that link, each is independent per the analysis rules. Fixed dollar floor: $130M for RP dividends.
• General RDP Basket [Section 6.09(a)(I)]: greater of $130M and 100% Consolidated EBITDA. This is an RDP basket (pay subordinated debt), not a direct dividend basket, but it is capacity_category = 'general_purpose' and action_scope = 'reallocable'. To the extent it can be reallocated to dividends, it represents an additional $130M floor. However, since the question is about dividend capacity specifically and this basket's primary scope is subordinated debt repayment (norm_scopes_action = pay_subordinated_debt), it is noted separately.
• Builder Basket [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32]: general_purpose, computed_from_sources. The Cumulative Amount is the SUM of: (i) greatest_of(50% cumulative CNI, Available Retained ECF Amount, 140% × Consolidated EBITDA for most recently ended Test Period) [the three-test aggregate per builder_source_three_test_aggregate] PLUS (ii) starter amount = greater of $130M and 100% Consolidated EBITDA PLUS (iii) 100% of equity proceeds received since Closing Date PLUS (iv) retained asset sale proceeds PLUS (v) investment returns PLUS (vi) debt conversion proceeds. The fixed quantifiable floor of the starter alone is $130M; the three-test aggregate's quantifiable floor is 140% × LTM EBITDA (the EBITDA floor component). The CNI and ECF components are variable and not quantifiable without financials.

Step 2 — Fungibility check: The general RP basket (6.06(j)) has capacity_composition = 'fungible' but no shares_capacity_pool link to any other basket is shown in the data. The general RDP basket has capacity_composition = 'additive'. No explicit shares_capacity_pool link exists between the RP and RDP general baskets. Treat as independent pools.

Step 3 — RESTRICTED_PURPOSE baskets (list separately, do NOT add to general total):
• Management Equity Basket [Section 6.06(b)]: greater of $20M p.a. and 15% of Consolidated EBITDA, with carryforward. Fixed floor: $20M per annum. Purpose-restricted to equity repurchases from employees/officers/directors.
• Tax Distribution Basket [Section 6.06(c)]: categorical, no dollar cap — not quantifiable.
• Holdco Overhead Basket [Section 6.06(k)]: categorical — not quantifiable.
• Equity Award Basket [Section 6.06(d)]: unlimited on condition (cashless exercise / tax withholding only) — not quantifiable.

Step 4 — UNLIMITED_CONDITIONAL baskets:
• Ratio RP Basket [Section 6.06(o)]: unlimited if First Lien Leverage Ratio ≤ 5.75x pro forma (or no-worse test). Not quantifiable in dollar terms — unlimited if condition met.

Step 5 — CATEGORICAL baskets:
• Unrestricted Sub Distribution Basket [Section 6.06(p)]: categorical, covers equity/assets of unsubs — not quantifiable without knowing unsub values.

Step 6 — Arithmetic for quantifiable fixed-dollar floors:
• General RP Basket: $130,000,000
• Builder Basket starter: $130,000,000 (this is a component of the builder basket's Cumulative Amount)
• Builder Basket three-test aggregate floor: The EBITDA floor component (140% × LTM EBITDA) is variable; CNI (50% cumulative) and ECF are variable. The greatest_of these three is taken — so the minimum quantifiable contribution from the three-test aggregate is $0 (if all are zero or negative, CNI and ECF are floored at $0 per the ECF formula). The fixed-dollar quantifiable floor of the three-test aggregate alone cannot be stated without financials.
• Management Equity Basket (restricted purpose): $20,000,000 p.a.

Conclusion on quantifiable capacity: The two independently quantifiable fixed-dollar general-purpose baskets with stated floors are: (1) General RP Basket at $130M floor [Section 6.06(j)] and (2) Builder Basket with a starter floor of $130M plus variable accumulation sources [Definition of Cumulative Amount]. These are separate general_purpose baskets with no shares_capacity_pool link, so they represent independent capacity pools. The total fixed-dollar floor for general-purpose dividend capacity is $130M (General RP Basket) plus the builder basket's Cumulative Amount (floored at $130M from the starter alone, growing with CNI/ECF/EBITDA/equity proceeds). The management equity basket adds a restricted-purpose $20M p.a. floor. The ratio basket adds unlimited capacity if leverage ≤ 5.75x.

The general RP basket (6.06(j)) and general RDP basket (6.09(a)(I)) both have the same $130M/100% EBITDA cap — the RDP basket is reallocable but its primary action scope is pay_subordinated_debt, not dividends. The builder RDP basket (6.09(a)(A)) shares its pool with the RP builder basket (shares_with_rp_builder=true), confirming the Cumulative Amount is a unified pool across RP and RDP uses. No defeaters are present.
reasoning.conclusion: Total quantifiable dividend capacity consists of: (1) a general-purpose General RP Basket floored at $130M (greater of $130M and 100% EBITDA) under Section 6.06(j); (2) a general-purpose Builder Basket (Cumulative Amount) with a fixed floor of at least $130M from the starter amount alone (greater of $130M and 100% EBITDA), growing by the greatest of 50% cumulative CNI, Available Retained ECF, and 140% of LTM EBITDA, plus 100% of equity proceeds and other additive sources — all variable and dependent on financial performance; (3) a restricted-purpose Management Equity Basket floored at $20M per annum under Section 6.06(b) (equity repurchases only); and (4) unlimited capacity under the ratio basket at Section 6.06(o) if First Lien Leverage Ratio is ≤ 5.75x pro forma. The minimum fixed-dollar floor for general-purpose dividend capacity (without financials) is $130M under the General RP Basket plus at least $130M under the Builder Basket starter = $260M in fixed-dollar floors, with substantial additional variable capacity.

The minimum fixed-dollar general-purpose floor (absent financial data) is $130M (General RP Basket) plus $130M (Builder starter) = $260M in aggregate fixed floors, each basket constituting an independent pool. reasoning.issue: Whether the Borrower can dividend a $200m asset/business division (with negative EBITDA) to shareholders when the First Lien Net Leverage Ratio is 6.0x, under the RP covenant's ratio basket and other available baskets.
reasoning.analysis: The primary ratio basket (Section 6.06(o)) permits unlimited dividends — including of a business division (scope_edge: norm_scopes_object → business_division) — subject to an OR condition: (1) pro forma First Lien Leverage Ratio ≤ 5.75x, OR (2) the 'no-worse' test (pro forma ratio does not exceed the pre-transaction ratio). At a current ratio of 6.0x, condition (1) fails because 6.0x > 5.75x. The critical question is whether condition (2) — the no-worse test — is satisfied. The business division has negative EBITDA. Removing a negative-EBITDA asset from the consolidated group on a pro forma basis would INCREASE consolidated EBITDA (negative contribution is removed), thereby REDUCING the First Lien Leverage Ratio. Therefore, the pro forma ratio after the dividend would be lower than the pre-transaction ratio of 6.0x, meaning the no-worse test IS satisfied. The ratio basket is available on this basis. Additionally, the general RP basket (Section 6.06(j)) provides up to the greater of $130m and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA — however, this is a dollar-capped basket for aggregate dividends and would need to cover the $200m FMV transaction; unless available capacity exceeds $200m (which depends on prior usage and current EBITDA), this basket alone may be insufficient. The builder basket (Section 6.06, Definition of Cumulative Amount) is general_purpose but requires available accumulated capacity. The unsub distribution basket (Section 6.06(p)) is categorical and unlimited but requires the asset/division to be held in an Unrestricted Subsidiary — not directly applicable if the division is a Restricted Subsidiary or direct asset. The ratio basket's no-worse test is the most reliable path given the negative-EBITDA division.
reasoning.interactions: The ratio basket (6e76ed06_ratio_rp_basket_permission) has partial_applicability=true and scopes business_division as an object, making it directly applicable. Its condition_tree is OR-structured: satisfying either the absolute threshold (5.75x) or the no-worse test is sufficient. No defeaters are listed. The general RP basket (6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission) is a separate general_purpose basket with its own dollar cap — it does not share a capacity pool with the ratio basket. The unsub distribution basket (6e76ed06_unrestricted_sub_equity_distribution_permission) is categorical with no dollar cap, but its object scope is limited to unrestricted_subsidiary_equity_or_assets.
reasoning.conclusion: The Borrower can dividend the negative-EBITDA business division to shareholders at 6.0x leverage by relying on the no-worse test under the ratio basket in Section 6.06(o), because removing a negative-EBITDA division improves (reduces) the pro forma First Lien Leverage Ratio relative to the 6.0x pre-transaction ratio, satisfying the OR condition even though the absolute 5.75x threshold is not met.

stage2.answer: Yes, the Borrower can dividend the $200m asset/business division to shareholders at a 6.0x First Lien Net Leverage Ratio by relying on the ratio basket in Section 6.06(o) [p.198], which permits unlimited dividends (including of a business division) if, on a pro forma basis, the First Lien Leverage Ratio is either (x) ≤ 5.75x OR (y) no worse than the pre-transaction ratio. While the 6.0x ratio fails the absolute 5.75x threshold, the no-worse test is satisfied: because the divested division carries negative EBITDA, removing it from the consolidated group increases pro forma Consolidated EBITDA and thereby reduces the pro forma First Lien Leverage Ratio below the pre-transaction 6.0x level. The ratio basket scopes 'business_division' as a permissible object and no defeaters are identified in the extracted data. As a fallback, the general RP basket under Section 6.06(j) [p.196] provides up to the greater of $130m and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA, which could cover a portion of the transaction depending on prior basket usage and current EBITDA levels.
